one_line_ask: Grade and consistently crop five raw café food/drink photos, vectorize the Hearthstone Brew Co. wordmark, license a warm kraft-paper texture, then run the client's authored InDesign café-menu template through a data-merge against the menu CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.
brief: Hearthstone Brew Co., a warm neighborhood café, needs its full food-and-drinks menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (hearthstone_menu_template.indd) laid out in a warm, cozy, elevated style — clean type, generous margins — with five photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>>) arranged under four section headers (Breakfast, All-Day Plates, Pastries, Coffee & Tea). Alongside it come the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, five raw phone-shot photos (a flat white, a stack of buttermilk pancakes, an avocado toast plate, a butter croissant, a cortado in a glass), and a CSV holding every menu row (section, item name, one-line description, price). The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull and flat, shot under cool café-window light with busy table clutter behind them. The job: (1) make every food/drink photo look warm and appetizing — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a cozy morning-light look, lift the shadows, push vibrance, apply a warm food-pop preset to lock a consistent look across all five, and throw the cluttered café background out of focus so each dish reads as
